description: Front-A conformance ledger for the platform-standards watch (backlog #1257 / #1267). One row per WE standard that has a SHIPPED native platform equivalent, tracking whether the standard yet registers that native API as its resolver impl (native-first, #031). The metric the gate surfaces is the count of rows where `registered: false` — WE standards that have a native equivalent but do not yet defer to it. The watch program maintains this ledger: each front-A registration item (e.g. #1261 popover) flips its row to `registered: true` and records the registration locus when it lands; each front-B run adds a row when a new native capability ships. Baseline is the cross-browser availability lens (widely | newly | limited | none). This is a curated tracking ledger, not a derived view — keep it honest at close-out of each watch item.

entries.standard: overlay / disclosure / dialog / tooltip / menu intents

entries.note: registered as an additional strategy; default promotion gated on Baseline (web-features baseline:false)

entries.standard: view-transitions protocol (#015, not yet materialized as a registry def)
entries.registeredIn: this ledger — the front-A native-first registry; the protocol has no materialized def to host a resolver field, and view-transitions is out of the droplist-substrate capabilityMatrix domain
entries.note: cross-document view transitions tracked separately as they advance

entries.standard: webscroll project (#014, not yet materialized as a registry def)
entries.registeredIn: this ledger — the front-A native-first registry; webscroll has no materialized def, and scroll-driven animation is out of the droplist-substrate capabilityMatrix domain
entries.note: baseline:false (web-features) — an Interop 2026 focus, maturing; JS scroll observers are the fallback. Default-promote when Baseline
